principles: YAGNI: ONE index kind (hierarchical Neo4j PropertyGraphIndex) + an extensible registry seam; no flat/FAISS index
Hierarchical chunk nodes (parent/child) are the default per user direction; small-to-big retrieval
links-to edges only; richer typed edges later
Benchmark suite removed completely from this branch; preserved on archive/pre-llamaindex-refactor; rebuilt later
Documentation/history cleanup is a first-class deliverable

verified_couplings.label_session.py: REMOVE — HITL tool over benchmark.jsonl; imports run_eval config helpers

tasks.title: Hierarchical chunker -> multi-level chunk nodes (parent/child)
tasks.notes: harness/indexing/chunking.py: chunk_document() uses HierarchicalNodeParser (keeps ALL levels, unlike old chunker.py:92), deterministic ids via post-parse remap (parent/child refs rewritten), is_leaf flag (no CHILD rel), metadata stamped (doc_id=sha256(url), source_url, title, committee, topic_path), excluded_embed/llm keys set, min_chunk_chars filter. leaf_nodes() helper. 7 unit tests pass; ruff clean.
acceptance_criteria: HierarchicalNodeParser produces multi-level chunk nodes (e.g. sizes [2048,512,128]); parent/child relationships retained (NOT discarded as in the old chunker.py:92)
Deterministic node_id per node (stable across rebuilds); leaf vs parent distinguishable
Each node carries source_url, doc_id, title, committee, topic_path, page (when known), level, chunk_index
Document->chunk (has_chunk) linkage represented
Unit test on a fixture asserts levels, parent/child links, ids, metadata

tasks.title: Link extractor -> links-to edges from parsed_documents/html_raw
tasks.notes: harness/indexing/links.py: extract_links(html, base_url) -> [ExtractedLink(tgt_url, anchor, kind)] where kind in file|page|external; resolves relative URLs (urljoin), strips fragments, skips mailto/tel/javascript/#/self-ref, dedups; tgt_doc_id=sha256(tgt_url). 6 unit tests pass; ruff clean. (This is the extractor MIGR-018..025 documented but never shipped — work unit 19.)
acceptance_criteria: Extractor produces (src_url -> tgt_url) links-to edges (this extractor did not exist before; see work unit 19)
Edges resolvable to doc_id = sha256(url) where the target exists in the corpus subset
mailto/tel/javascript/#fragment/self-references skipped
Unit test on an HTML fixture asserts extracted edges

tasks.title: Ingestion: Mongo -> LlamaIndex node/edge graph (subset, fidelity)
tasks.notes: harness/indexing/ingest.py: ingest(profile) reads parsed_documents (error=''), dedups one row/url, derives metadata via url_metadata/text_metadata, chunks (hierarchical), extracts links_to from web_items.html_raw for HTML docs; returns list[IngestedDoc(doc_id,source_url,source_type,title,metadata,chunk_nodes,links)]. Subset scope: topic_prefix+limit (URL-level), committee (post-derivation). 5 mongomock tests pass (dedup/skip/metadata/links/limit/committee); full indexing suite 34 tests green; ruff clean. Also hardened chunking.py: Document excluded_*_metadata_keys so the splitter ignores provenance metadata. mongomock installed in conda base.
acceptance_criteria: Reads Mongo parsed_documents -> entity nodes (page/pdf) + hierarchical chunk nodes + has_chunk + parent/child + links-to
Subset scope filter honored (committee / topic_path / url / doc cap) from the profile

tasks.title: Build hierarchical Neo4j PropertyGraphIndex (persist + vector index, idempotent)
tasks.result: harness/indexing/property_graph.py: to_graph() maps IngestedDoc IR -> EntityNode(Document) + ChunkNode + Relation(HAS_CHUNK/PARENT_OF/LINKS_TO); build_property_graph_index embeds chunks (bge-large) + upserts to Neo4j + creates a dedicated Chunk vector index (ema_chunk_embedding, 1024-d cosine); from_existing uses MockLLM (no OpenAI). VERIFIED LIVE on Neo4j (7688) with an 8-doc/49-chunk slice: build OK, retrieval returns relevant chunks with scores + source_url. 2 to_graph unit tests; full indexing suite 36 tests pass; ruff clean. NOTE: full 40-doc build deferred — CPU embed ~0.7s/chunk => ~17 min for 1462 chunks (run build_index(reset=True) in background, or on GPU).
tasks.blocker_resolved: Chose (a): scripts/backfill_parsed_documents_subset.py populated 40 parsed_documents (12 HTML via trafilatura + 28 linked PDFs copied from parsed_pdfs) into the live native mongod (:27017). Coherent subset (HTML pages chosen by their resolvable PDF links) so links_to lands in-subset. Remaining for LIR-007: write harness/indexing/property_graph.py (build the PropertyGraphIndex over Neo4j from the ingest IR — entity+chunk nodes + has_chunk/parent-child/links_to + leaf-chunk embeddings + Neo4j vector index), download bge-large, run the 40-doc build, verify the graph. Coupled to the LIR-008 small-to-big retriever spike.
tasks.note_data: parsed_documents/link_graph were absent on this host; backfilled a 40-doc verify subset. Native mongod active => pinned container stays off (corruption guard).
tasks.defect_spun_out_2026-06-02: LIR-007's build embeds ALL hierarchical chunk levels, not just leaves (missing is_leaf filter in property_graph._embed_pass). Live graph: 269,112/1,285,159 chunks are non-leaf and ALL embedded. The splitter (HierarchicalNodeParser) is correct; the bug is ours. Investigation + fix plan: work unit 2026-06-02_21_leaf-only-embedding (LOE-001..004). Affects retrieval quality (mixed-granularity vector index breaks small-to-big) + ~21% wasted embed compute. Fix is independent of the EPAR-scope question.
tasks.full_build_2026-06-02: Full-corpus GPU build LAUNCHED. parsed_documents now holds 80,083 clean docs in the mongo:8.0.4 container (:27017) — native mongod is down, container is the live source. Added harness/indexing/build.py CLI + batched/resumable streaming build in property_graph.py (flush every --flush-chunks, resume skips docs with >=1 chunk) + a GPU throttle (--pause-every-docs/--pause-seconds: flush then sleep, crash-safe). nvidia driver reinstalled, power capped 250W; the 60s/1000-doc pause mitigates the GSP-wedge-under-sustained-load failure (root cause UNCONFIRMED, NOT kernel-specific — user saw the same crash on Ubuntu 24.04). Resume cmd: HF_HUB_OFFLINE=1 .venv/bin/python -m harness.indexing.build --full --embed-device cuda --pause-every-docs 1000 --pause-seconds 60. Neo4j had 300 docs built pre-launch; run skips those. Verified throttle live (49-doc/166-chunk slice, pause fired). Log: logs/build_full_*.log.
acceptance_criteria: INDEX_REGISTRY['property_graph'] materializes entity + hierarchical chunk nodes + edges (has_chunk, parent/child, links-to) into Neo4jPropertyGraphStore
Leaf-chunk embeddings written; Neo4j native vector index created and queryable
Idempotent re-build (no duplicate nodes/edges; stable ids)
Subset graph verified in Neo4j (entity/chunk/level counts, parent/child + links-to edges present)

tasks.title: Hierarchical retriever (small-to-big merge + links-to traversal) + default profile
tasks.spike_outcome: Small-to-big does NOT need AutoMergingRetriever. The default VectorContextRetriever searches Neo4j's `entity` index (__Entity__ nodes only) — useless for our chunk-centric model. Resolved with a custom HierarchicalPGRetriever(BaseRetriever): embeds the query, runs db.index.vector.queryNodes over the dedicated Chunk vector index, then in ONE Cypher expands HAS_CHUNK (-> source_url/doc_id) + PARENT_OF (-> parent text) and returns the parent chunk when merge=True (small-to-big), deduped. Verified live: relevant hits + provenance for 2 queries. Default profile neo4j_hier wired.
acceptance_criteria: SPIKE FIRST (timeboxed): determine how small-to-big merge composes with PropertyGraphIndex/Neo4j — native AutoMergingRetriever (needs parents in a docstore) vs a custom parent-merge over PG-retriever results via parent/child edges. Record the choice in the work unit.
RETRIEVER_REGISTRY['property_graph'] returns a LlamaIndex BaseRetriever: vector search on leaf chunks -> merge up via parent/child -> 1-hop links-to expansion
harness/configs/index/neo4j_hier.yaml is the working default profile
Retriever returns NodeWithScore with full metadata; smoke test on the subset shows merged parent context (not scattered leaves)

tasks.title: Re-seam workflows to a LlamaIndex retriever; metadata-based citations
acceptance_criteria: get_workflow + all kept strategies (simple_rag, crag, react, summarize_rag, composites, review) consume a retriever/query engine
Tuple retrieve_fn path and the dual 'index' parameter removed from the workflow seam
Citations derived from node metadata (doc_id/source_url/chunk), replacing cited_qa_ids
Each kept workflow runs end-to-end on the subset retriever; review workflow's judge call still works
tasks.notes: DONE (offline-validated). Re-seamed all workflows to consume a LlamaIndex BaseRetriever: get_workflow(retriever=...), each strategy calls retriever.aretrieve() -> nodes_from_retrieval(); dropped the dual index/retrieve_fn/RetrievalConfig path + ablation_config span attrs (utils.retriever_attributes instead). format_docs + citations key on node metadata (source_url/doc_id) not qa_id. ReAct toolset reduced to ema_search + filter_by_topic (FAISS-backed follow_cross_refs/get_qa_by_id removed). 29 workflow tests rewritten to a fake retriever + green; 427-test collection clean; ruff clean. review/judge chain intact. Live end-to-end verification is LIR-011 (post-build).
